issues.criterion: F-001/AT: ROADMAP.md marks phase 005 as the active plan of record (not merely candidate scope).
issues.evidence: Verification entry 7's command is `grep -Eqi '005' docs/planning/ROADMAP.md`. Re-ran this exact command in a disposable git worktree checked out at HEAD (f5a6dc4, before any of this feature's deliverables exist) and it PASSES there too: `grep -niE '005' docs/planning/ROADMAP.md` at HEAD matches six pre-existing lines, including `line 11: ... 'the phase 005 candidate scope.'`, `line 14: ... 'a debt-driven phase 005 candidate scope ...'`, `line 56: '## Track 2 -- Phase 005 Candidate Scope: Consumer Reality & Enforcement Closure'`, and `line 122/124/126` ('Track 2 is the recommended phase 005 ...'). None of these is the Plan-of-Record banner this feature is supposed to add -- they are exactly the pre-existing 'candidate scope' references the acceptance criterion explicitly says must be superseded ('not merely candidate scope'). Because the bare case-insensitive substring '005' already occurs in the untouched, pre-implementation ROADMAP.md, this command passes identically whether or not the generator ever writes the 'Plan of Record (2026-07-17)' banner described in scope.files_modify[1]. A no-op (or wrong) implementation that changes nothing in ROADMAP.md, or changes something unrelated, still yields PASS. This is the same false-pass/false-fail class methodology/07A guards against for the Test Engineer's own suite; here it means the contract's own criterion 7 does not discriminate a correct implementation from a missing one -- it is not load-bearing.
issues.required_fix: Replace the bare `grep -Eqi '005'` with a check anchored to the actual required content (e.g. grep for the literal banner text the contract's own scope.files_modify[1] specifies, such as `Plan of Record \(2026-07-17\)`, or a phrase asserting phase 005 is ACTIVE rather than candidate, e.g. a case-sensitive match on `Plan of Record` combined with `005-ecosystem-cycle` or the frontmatter version bump 0.2.0->0.3.0/change_log entry). Additionally, per the entry-13 precedent (which independently re-confirms manifest.json's HEAD state to prove entry 4 non-vacuous), add a companion anti-vacuity entry that re-confirms HEAD's docs/planning/ROADMAP.md does NOT already contain the tightened marker, so the fixed entry 7 is proven non-vacuous the same way entry 4/13 is.

issues.criterion: non_goals: "No edit to .agent/run_state.json, any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 031.json, any .agent/qa/*.json, or any prior feature_list_00N.json -- those are immutable audit records or owned by the orchestrator"
issues.evidence: None of the 13 verification entries checks this non-goal. Entry 9 (the untracked-aware scope guard, `vlib_scope_guard`) is invoked against `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- . ':(exclude).agent'` -- it explicitly EXCLUDES the entire .agent/ tree from its scan, so it cannot ever catch a historical .agent/contracts/*.json, .agent/qa/*.json, or prior feature_list_00N.json edit. This repository has an established, precedented pattern for exactly this immutability guard: contracts 025 and 028 (`.agent/contracts/025.json` verification entry, `.agent/contracts/028.json` verification entry AT9-revised) both carry a dedicated entry of the form `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts ':(exclude).agent/contracts/0NN.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/0NN*.json'`, failing if any historical .agent/qa or .agent/contracts file was touched. Re-ran that exact pattern substituting 031 against the live tree just now: it PASSES currently (no historical .agent file is touched at contract-authoring time), confirming the check is technically satisfiable and would have been a legitimate, precedented addition -- its absence is a real coverage gap for Loop 2, not a hypothetical one. Because the contract's own design_notes flags .agent/run_state.json as an out-of-band orchestrator concern and NOT reviewed here, and because prior feature_list_00N.json files (001-004) are equally immutable audit records under this same non-goal, a Loop-2 implementation that (accidentally or otherwise) edits .agent/contracts/030.json, .agent/qa/029.json, or .agent/feature_list_004.json during 031's 'verification pass' would be caught by none of the 13 commands.
issues.required_fix: Add a 025/028-precedented immutability-guard verification entry scoped to `.agent/qa` and `.agent/contracts` (and, if desired, prior `feature_list_00N.json` paths), excluding only this feature's own new `.agent/contracts/031.json` and `.agent/qa/031*.json` artifacts via pathspec exclusion, mirroring: `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts ':(exclude).agent/contracts/031.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/031*.json'` must be empty.

missing_acceptance_coverage: Feature 031's acceptance test text ('NIP body records the Accepted decision, the operator, the 2026-07-17 date, and the verbatim authorization') explicitly requires the operator identity to be recorded, but verification entry 3's command only asserts three substrings ('Accepted', '2026-07-17', 'approved. expedite.') are present anywhere in the file -- it never checks for an operator field/identity (e.g. 'operator', an email, or a named role). The real docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md file does record an operator ('Ecosystem operator (`rosscn@nizamiq.com`)') in its Acceptance Record table, so the current artifact happens to satisfy the prose criterion -- but no verification entry would catch a revision that dropped the operator field while keeping the other three substrings, so this half of the stated acceptance criterion has no independent proof.
observations: All 13 verification commands were extracted programmatically from .agent/contracts/031.json (not retyped) and re-run verbatim from the repository root against the live, uncommitted working tree: all 13 PASS, exactly as design_notes.nature_of_this_feature claims (re-confirmed independently, not merely trusted).

Built a disposable git worktree at HEAD (f5a6dc4, `git worktree add /tmp.../031-negctrl HEAD`) to test anti-vacuity of the doc-existence/content entries pre-implementation, then removed it (`git worktree remove --force`) and confirmed `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` on the real tree is unchanged (same 5 untracked + 3 modified paths as before this review started). At HEAD: entry 1 (NIP file exists) correctly FAILS (file absent); entry 4 (manifest indexes phase 005) correctly FAILS; .agent/feature_list_005.json is confirmed absent (entry 6 would correctly fail via FileNotFoundError before jsonschema.validate ever runs); entry 8 (`bash tools/validate.sh`) still reports `SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed` at HEAD -- expected, since it is a pure regression check (this docs-only feature is not expected to change C1-C11) and not meant to discriminate implementation state, so this is NOT a vacuity defect. Entry 7, uniquely, ALSO passes at HEAD -- this is the finding logged as issue #1 above (high severity, blocking).

Confirmed entries 9 and 11 are genuinely load-bearing (not just documentation) via disposable positive/negative probes on the live tree, reverted immediately: (a) creating a throwaway untracked file `bogus_out_of_scope_probe.txt` and re-running entry 9's `vlib_scope_guard` invocation correctly FAILS with `vlib_scope_guard: out-of-scope changed path: bogus_out_of_scope_probe.txt`; removed the probe file afterward, `git status --porcelain` confirms no residue. (b) appending a byte to `schema/manifest.schema.json` and re-running entry 11's `git diff --quiet HEAD -- NIZAM.json schema/ tools/ ecosystem/` correctly FAILS; reverted via `git checkout -- schema/manifest.schema.json`, confirmed byte-identical to HEAD afterward.

Entry 13 is a genuinely load-bearing, well-constructed anti-vacuity check for entry 4 -- it independently re-derives (via `git show HEAD:...`) that the pre-implementation manifest.json does not already index phase 005, closing exactly the same class of defect found in entry 7. No equivalent anti-vacuity companion exists for entry 7 (see issue #1) or for entries 1/2/3/6/10 (file-existence-gated entries), though those five are inherently non-vacuous already because the files they check did not exist at all prior to this dispatch (confirmed above), unlike entry 7's bare substring match against an already-populated document.

The `ecosystem/README.md` file referenced by non_goals and verification entry 11 already exists at HEAD (committed by a prior, out-of-scope commit `f5a6dc4 'introduce ecosystem engineering cycle module'`, 12 lines) -- this predates and is unrelated to contract 031; entry 11 correctly treats it as part of the protected byte-identical set and it is untouched by the current working-tree diff. Not a defect; noted only so the orchestrator is aware `ecosystem/` is not empty at HEAD.

The pre-existing untracked `v0.6.0-release-notes.md` (called out in design_notes.pre_existing_untracked_file_out_of_scope and tolerated in entry 9's allow-list) is independently confirmed to predate this feature: it is untracked at HEAD, is not part of scope.files_create/files_modify, and no verification entry authorizes editing it -- consistent with the contract's own framing.

Contract 031 is schema-valid: `python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('.agent/contracts/031.json')), json.load(open('schema/contract.schema.json')))"` exits 0 (re-run as part of this review, not merely inferred).

final_verdict.summary: REJECTED at revision 0. Twelve of the thirteen verification commands are sound, non-vacuous, and load-bearing -- independently re-run (not trusted from the contract's own claims) against both the live working tree (all 13 currently PASS) and a disposable HEAD worktree (the file-existence/content entries 1, 2, 4, 6, and 10 correctly FAIL pre-implementation; entry 8 is a legitimate regression-only check, not required to discriminate; entries 9 and 11 were proven load-bearing via reverted positive/negative probes; entry 13 is a well-built anti-vacuity companion for entry 4). However, verification entry 7 (`grep -Eqi '005' docs/planning/ROADMAP.md`) is a genuine, reproduced false-pass: the bare substring '005' already occurs six times in ROADMAP.md at HEAD via pre-existing 'phase 005 candidate scope' references (lines 11, 14, 56, 122, 124, 126) -- exactly the language the acceptance criterion says must be superseded by an active Plan-of-Record banner. This means entry 7 cannot distinguish a correct implementation from a no-op, and the acceptance criterion it is meant to prove ('ROADMAP.md marks phase 005 as the active plan of record, not merely candidate scope') currently has NO non-vacuous verification. Additionally, the contract's own non-goal protecting .agent/run_state.json, prior .agent/contracts/*.json, .agent/qa/*.json, and prior feature_list_00N.json files from edits has zero verification coverage (entry 9's scope guard explicitly excludes .agent/ from its scan), a gap relative to this repository's own established precedent (contracts 025 and 028 both carry a dedicated .agent/qa+.agent/contracts immutability guard). A minor, non-blocking coverage gap was also found: entry 3 does not independently verify the 'operator' identity the acceptance criterion text requires, though the real file happens to satisfy it. Required before re-review: (1) tighten or replace entry 7 with a marker anchored to the actual Plan-of-Record content, plus an entry-13-style anti-vacuity companion; (2) add the precedented .agent/qa + .agent/contracts immutability guard entry. Not required to block, but recommended: strengthen entry 3 to also check for an operator-identity marker.
